рЯІ† AIвАЩs Crypto News Brief: Bitcoin remains volatile as geopolitical tensions pressure prices, but ETF inflows and institutional buying continue supporting the market. Tokenized equities gain momentum with new infrastructure launches, while Paradigm expands into AI with a $1.2B fund. Polymarket prepares its U.S. comeback after years of regulatory scrutiny. рЯУИ¬†BitcoinвАЩs ETF comeback is relying on a $79B futures market betting the rebound holds Futures volume is running far ahead of spot demand, making absorption the next test for BTC's recovery. The post BitcoinвАЩs ETF comeback is relying on a $79B futures market betting the rebound holds appeared first on CryptoSlate.¬†More details рЯУЙ¬†Polymarket bets on U.S. marketing blitz to win back trust after 4-year ban: Report The prediction market's head of U.S. operations says the business is taking steps to legitimize itself after years of legal scrutiny.¬†Read more рЯУИ¬†Bitcoin slides as Iran ceasefire collapse sees $75 oil on Hormuz blockade threats Bitcoin price pressure took BTC toward the "crucial" $61,000 mark as oil prices soared on the collapse of the US-Iran ceasefire.¬†More details рЯТ≤¬†Crypto Long & Short: With MSTR concerns assuaged, look to traditional signals around BTC In this week's Crypto Long & Short, FalconXвАЩs Gaspar Martin writes that BTC is reaching a market bottom and is poised for a turnaround.¬†Explore further рЯ§Ц¬†Crypto VC Paradigm launches $1.2 billion AI fund as it broadens beyond digital assets: BBG The firm's latest fund backs AI and robotics startups but leadership says it remains committed to crypto investing.¬†More info рЯФС¬†Dinari, tZERO join forces on turnkey platform for tokenized U.S. equities The move comes as firms race to build the infrastructure for blockchain-based stocks and debate how tokenized stocks should work.¬†More details рЯУИ¬†Bitcoin ETF Inflows Return As Farside Data Shows Institutions Still Buying The Dip BitcoinвАЩs supply headlines have been loud, but ETF flow data is giving bulls something to point to. FarsideвАЩs numbers show a $143 million net inflow day for US spot Bitcoin ETFs, suggesting institutional buyers are still¬†Explore further Stay ahead in crypto. 75656.subject: EuropeвАЩs economy is a mess. Its stock markets are a steal
75656.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzF9AAcAAAAAA7uEMD3xMlrGM92fW83tugFNr-t-vSudI_tXxUjj4UrvounYLjA7YvZ9MmAs4BCoa1NU7ggOM6pFgr2N_eK-SsGJmjnTkQYrvQy8Dg4SFIGIww 

July 8th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzF9AAcAAAAAA7uEMD3y7hxBYBIRloA4aegWbwISQW9cyFdK-CrwwJ3xijnZxTVoIoTEYxXkHwOOijzo7467Y3ymNwDM6sjFzn4y3v_ay2LazM7cA0-9lp0W9A 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzF9AAcAAAAAA7uEMD3z-2tqCB95gNFVM3upxMYbg8rYw6DwLLEv2wvp8wdDZo2zAWsJkE5Kx28b_faHmWUZZsULBT36zEeEs2ijlPpaId8OVRNmPQQUAzIYbQ 


 


 
 

Bo Franklin

Senior digital editor



Europe’s economy has little going for it. GDP growth is sluggish compared with much of the world, the continent lacks the AI giants of America and China (and data centres to support them), and its energy markets are vulnerable to wars and other disruptions. But, more than anywhere else in the world, Europe’s stock markets are not its economy, 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzF9AAcAAAAAA7uEMD30xy0WCxONx9kRuKWAFIiWwqnCeu3kMtggwED0stIrHrnvoOmf_KUngw-9a8394u2xC4YtDR5QSbmUB2-7OlFEw6N8M2bxR6nq 
argues Buttonwood . Its shares deserve a lot more affection.


Nigel Farage hopes to weather a parliamentary investigation and reinvigorate the prospects of Reform UK, his party, by calling (and winning) a by-elec

75652.subject: The State of NEAR: Q2 2026
75652.body:
NEAR Protocol Monthly Update

–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: NEAR Protocol


–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: NEAR Protocol

    
    
    –Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞–є—В–µ —Ж—О —Б—В–∞—В—В—О –љ–∞ LinkedIn, —Й–Њ–± –њ—А–Є—Ф–і–љ–∞—В–Є—Б—П –і–Њ –Њ–±–≥–Њ–≤–Њ—А–µ–љ–љ—П
–Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞—В–Є –љ–∞ LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/state-near-q2-2026-near-protocol-project-7npgc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Bpj6lI%2Fo6SryE4ZBJhH7R4g%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=096seEBXvzAIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrc8j35n~av-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrc8j35n-av
  
TL;DR The Revenue Engine Is Already Running and Accelerating: Protocol fee capture has scaled from near-zero to over 30% of fees in a matter of months, with a meaningful share of all lifetime net revenue earned in the most recent period. Live and verifiable at revenue.near.org. near.com Is a Key Revenue Lever in the Stack: NEARвАЩs native frontend captures 100% of its fees and routes them to NEAR buybacks. Volume and fee generation have grown several-fold since April, ahead of internal targets. Every percentage point of total Intents flow that shifts toward near.com improves the blended capture rate across the entire protocol. Confidentiality Is Becoming Default Behavior: A large and growing share of near.com swap volume now runs confidential, with confidential TVL past $30 million.¬† Deflation Is a Measurable Threshold, Not a Narrative: Net deflation is a defined function of protocol revenue against issuance, and the threshold is already being approached, and crossed on certain high-volume days, before any cyclical tailwind. NEAR Protocol Is Getting Significantly More Powerful: Dynamic resharding and post-quantum cryptography just went live on testnet, making NEAR one of the first major L1s with one-click PQ key rotation and hardware wallet support from day one.¬† The Next Wave Extends Beyond Today's Fundamentals: NEAR's current traction rests largely on Intents. The agent market, confidential inference, IronClaw, and the institutional volume ramp represent a separate layer of potential value accrual that sits on top of an engine that is already working. Most foundational technology bets spend years looking early. The ones that were right eventually reach a point where the thesis and the traction converge, and it becomes difficult to argue against. In 2017, Illia Polosukhin was a machine learning researcher at Google when he co-authored a paper that would quietly rewrite the trajectory of the entire technology industry.¬† That same year, he and Alex Skidanov, who had built one of the first production-grade distributed databases at MemSQL, joined forces with a specific goal: build AI models that could write code from natural language descriptions. In attempting to pay global contributors for that training data, they ran into a coordination problem that existing financial infrastructure couldn't solve at scale. The answer was blockchain. NEAR
